description: Microsoft VibeVoice-Realtime 0.5B (MIT) as an MLX 4bit TTS checkpoint: streaming-style text input, ~300 ms class first audible latency (hardware dependent), long-form speech (~10 min class, 8k context), built on Qwen2.5-0.5B + 7.5 Hz acoustic tokenizer. English-first; extra languages are experimental upstream.

notes: VibeVoice-Realtime focuses on low-latency research TTS; follow upstream responsible-use guidance
Optional extra speaker bundles: see microsoft/VibeVoice demo/download_experimental_voices.sh for multilingual exploration voices
Long-form multi-speaker VibeVoice-TTS (≈1.5B) is separate; upstream removed TTS training/inference code from GitHub in Sept 2025 — see README “Important update”

modelCard.summary: MIT Realtime VibeVoice line (0.5B): streaming-oriented TTS suitable for assistant prototypes; MLX 4bit port from microsoft/VibeVoice-Realtime-0.5B.
useCases: Local Flutter TTS demos paired with an LM
Long single-speaker narration experiments (~10 min class per upstream)
limitations: Research-only intent per Microsoft model card; disclose AI-generated speech
English-trained; other languages may be unstable unless you add upstream experimental voice assets
Not a drop-in replacement for long-form 4-speaker VibeVoice-TTS (different architecture & availability)
Voice IDs depend on checkpoint packaging — use Session settings to try documented presets (e.g. mlx example en-Emma_woman)

voice.description: Voice id — filename without .safetensors from the voices/ subdirectory.

extra.experimental_voice_pack: demo/download_experimental_voices.sh in microsoft/VibeVoice (multilingual / extra English styles per project news)
extra.tts_upstream_note: VibeVoice-TTS 1.5B long-form: HF entry may show disabled/research-only; upstream removed public TTS code from this GitHub org (Sept 2025). Prefer Realtime 0.5B here or follow current README tables.
extra.vibevoice_asr_sibling: Pair with catalog model vibevoice-asr-4bit for MLX ASR from the same research line when you need speech-to-text.

description: Microsoft VibeVoice-ASR (MIT) as an MLX 4bit speech-to-text checkpoint: unified long-form ASR with diarization/timestamp features at 7B class scale (converted by mlx-community from microsoft/VibeVoice-ASR). Suitable for high-quality offline transcription experiments on Apple Silicon.

notes: Large VibeVoice ASR checkpoint (~5.7 GB weights); allow plenty of unified memory
Uses mlx-audio STT pipeline (`python -m mlx_audio.stt.generate --model mlx-community/VibeVoice-ASR-4bit`)
Jan 2026: VibeVoice-ASR integrates with Hugging Face Transformers per microsoft/VibeVoice news — this catalog entry tracks the MLX port

modelCard.summary: MIT VibeVoice-ASR line in MLX 4bit: long-form multilingual speech recognition with rich metadata (speaker / timestamps / content) at the source project; mlx-community conversion for local Studio tests.
useCases: Offline transcription for voice-pipeline and QA fixtures
Long-form meeting audio experiments (up to ~60 min class context at source)
Pairing with VibeVoice Realtime TTS or other local assistants
limitations: Much heavier than Whisper tiny/base—expect long load times and high RAM use
Studio passes generic STT parameters; advanced hotword / structured JSON modes need manual mlx-audio usage
Research intent per upstream; verify licenses and responsible-use text before shipping

extra.hf_transformers_note: 2026-03: microsoft/VibeVoice README notes Transformers integration for VibeVoice-ASR — Studio uses mlx-audio native STT path
